title: Realm Peril
description: One terse gazetteer line on a realm's standing danger — for rollable realm entries.
entries: Wyvern eyries in the high crags; the shepherd villages pay a tithe in mutton and count it cheap.
A bandit confederacy in the border forest, taxed once a year by the crown in lieu of catching it.
The old barrow downs are avoided by daylight and forbidden by night; the wardens' pay reflects the honesty of the arrangement.
River pirates below the last cataract; convoys sail in threes, and insurance rates are a form of news.
A slow plague of the grain — a black smut that crosses no river, which tells the scholars something they can't yet use.
Wolves in hard winters, in numbers the old songs got right; the bounty doubles after midwinter.
An undead march where a battlefield never emptied; the realm plows around it and posts the anniversary.
Giants in the high pasture, lawful by their own lights; the misunderstandings average one burned village a decade.
A dragon, dormant these forty years, on which every budget quietly turns; the tribute line is labeled 'mountain maintenance'.
Sea raiders each spring tide; the coast towns build their churches square, with arrow slits.
The mists off the fen carry travelers away — some return years later, unaged and unhelpful.
A rift in the waste that leaks horrors at intervals no one has mapped; the watch-forts are the realm's most honored posting.
Cattle-raiding across the border, answered in kind; both crowns tax the practice rather than end it.
Earthquakes along the old fault; the code requires timber frames, and stone churches are lately unfashionable.
A cursed road nobody closes because it's the only flat one; carters go in convoy behind a paid priest.
Trolls under three bridges of record; the tolls are paid in livestock and the arrangement is centuries stable.
Slavers' galleys over the horizon; the realm's patrol sloops are fast, angry, and never quite enough.
A witch-court in the deep wood that honors old treaties and enforces them exactly; the realm's lawyers dread its literalism.
Sinkholes that open overnight and swallow fields whole; the survey office redraws freeholds quarterly.
Locust years on a seven-year rhythm; the granary law dates from the third famine and is enforced like blasphemy.
A rival pretender across the water, with a court, a seal, and patient correspondents inside the realm.
Manticores denning in the quarry country; the stone convoys hire lancers and the price of marble includes them.
The forest is growing — measurably, yearly, against the axe; the border villages have begun to negotiate with it.
Fire in the coal seams, burning underground for a generation; whole districts are named for their smokes.
Ghouls in the catacombs of the old capital; the necropolis guild's monopoly on entry is grim, profitable, and strictly kept.
Avalanche country; the horns are sounded by law before any loud work, and weddings are quiet affairs.
A god sleeps under the lake, say the fisherfolk, who will not fish its center; the navy charts agree with them without comment.
Harpies foul the cliff roads each nesting season; the coast post reroutes inland and the letters run late.
An assassin's guild older than the dynasty, lawful within its ancient charter; the premiums for charter work are public record.
Blight-rains from the waste when the wind sits wrong; the alarm bells ring and the realm moves indoors with practiced speed.
Basilisk sign in the dry hills; the goat-herds carry mirrors and the insurance carries exclusions.
Nothing, its neighbors would say, but the realm itself; it is the peril in everyone else's entry.
